steps.title: Connect alloy to use
steps.content: Ferritic stainless steel is used for cooking utensils and has the high iron concentration responsible for magnetic properties. Mining and chemical equipment use 300-series austenitic stainless steel, which is defined by its nickel-derived crystalline structure.

steps.title: Eliminate unsupported traits
steps.content: Choice B makes an unsupported chromium claim about washing machines. Choice C gives cooking utensils the austenitic crystalline structure. Choice D wrongly links compensators with the ferritic structure used for cooking utensils. The remaining inference follows from the alloy descriptions. The answer is A.
